controls.control_objective: To ensure be called the reserve bank of india (small finance banks - prudential norms on capital adequacy) directions, 2025
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks
- Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be called the reserve bank of india (small finance banks - prudential norms on capital adequacy) directions, 2025

controls.control_objective: To ensure come into effect immediately upon issuance
controls.control_description: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to come into effect immediately upon issuance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applicable to small finance banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'sfbs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'sfb')
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be applicable to Small Finance Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'SFBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'SFB').
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applicable to small finance banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'sfbs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'sfb')

controls.control_objective: To ensure refer to all applicable extant statutory and regulatory directions and requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an activity
controls.control_description:
Note: Mere mention of an activity, transaction or item in these Directions does
not imply that it is permitted bank shall refer to all applicable extant statutory and
regulatory Directions and requirements while determining the permissibility or
otherwise of an activity, transaction or item.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to refer to all applicable extant statutory and regulatory directions and requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an activity

controls.control_objective: To ensure bear the meanings assigned to them below: (1) 'banking book' shall mean all items which are not included under trading book as per these directions
controls.control_description:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Banking book' shall mean all items which are not included under trading book
as per these Directions;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to bear the meanings assigned to them below: (1) 'banking book' shall mean all items which are not included under trading book as per these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the same meaning as defined in reserve bank of india (small finance banks - concentration risk management) directions, 2025
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the same meaning as defined in reserve bank of india (small finance banks - concentration risk management) directions, 2025

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as tranches
controls.control_description:
A CCP becomes counterparty to trades
with market participants through novation, an open offer system, or another
Explanation - Securitisation notes issued by the SPE and credit enhancement
facilities available shall be treated as tranches;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as tranches

controls.control_objective: To ensure the tranche's effective maturity in years and is measured as prescribed in paragraphs 97 to 99 of these directions
controls.control_description:
(31) 'Tranche Maturity' means the tranche's effective maturity in years and is
measured as prescribed in Paragraphs 97 to 99 of these Directions;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the tranche's effective maturity in years and is measured as prescribed in paragraphs 97 to 99 of these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure the measure calculated as detachment point (d) minus attachment point (a)
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the measure calculated as detachment point (d) minus attachment point (a)

controls.control_objective: To ensure have a board approved policy on the following matters pertaining to capital adequacy:
controls.control_description:
A bank shall have a Board approved policy on the following matters pertaining to
capital adequacy:
controls.implementation_method: Establish and maintain policy to have a board approved policy on the following matters pertaining to capital adequacy:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be approved by the board of directors to ensure that the icaap forms an integral part of the management process and decision-making culture of a bank
controls.control_description:
(i) The structure, design and contents of a bank's Internal Capital Adequacy
Assessment Process (ICAAP) should be approved by the Board of
Directors to ensure that the ICAAP forms an integral part of the
management process and decision-making culture of a bank;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be approved by the board of directors to ensure that the icaap forms an integral part of the management process and decision-making culture of a bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure have an explicit board-approved capital plan which should spell out the institution's objectives in regard to level of capital, the time horizon for a
controls.control_description:
(ii) A bank shall have an explicit Board-approved capital plan which should
spell out the institution's objectives in regard to level of capital, the time
horizon for achieving those objectives, and in broad terms, the capital
planning process, and the allocated responsibilities for that process;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have an explicit board-approved capital plan which should spell out the institution's objectives in regard to level of capital, the time horizon for a

controls.control_objective: To ensure have a formal disclosure policy approved by the board of directors that addresses a bank's approach for determining what disclosures it shall make and
controls.control_description:
(iii) A bank shall have a formal disclosure policy approved by the Board of
Directors that addresses a bank's approach for determining what
disclosures it shall make and the internal controls over the disclosure
process.
controls.implementation_method: Establish and maintain policy to have a formal disclosure policy approved by the board of directors that addresses a bank's approach for determining what disclosures it shall make and

controls.control_objective: To ensure assess and document, at least once a year, whether the processes relating to the icaap implemented by a bank successfully achieve the objectives envis
controls.control_description:
A bank's Board of Directors shall assess and document, at least once a year,
whether the processes relating to the ICAAP implemented by a bank successfully
achieve the objectives envisaged by the Board.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to assess and document, at least once a year, whether the processes relating to the icaap implemented by a bank successfully achieve the objectives envis

controls.control_objective: To ensure be based on three components or three pillars
controls.control_description:
The capital adequacy framework shall be based on three components or three
Pillars.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be based on three components or three pillars

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain a minimum pillar 1 capital to risk- weighted assets ratio (crar) of 15 per cent on an on-going basis as prescribed under these directions
controls.control_description:
A bank shall maintain a minimum Pillar 1 Capital to Risk-
weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 15 per cent on an on-going basis as prescribed
under these Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain a minimum pillar 1 capital to risk- weighted assets ratio (crar) of 15 per cent on an on-going basis as prescribed under these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure compute its capital ratios in the following manner: cet1 capital common equity tier 1 (cet1) capital ratio = total risk weighted assets (rwas) eligibl
controls.control_description:
A bank shall compute its capital ratios in the
following manner:
CET1 capital
Common Equity Tier 1
(CET1) capital ratio =
Total Risk Weighted Assets (RWAs)
Eligible Tier 1 capital
Tier 1 capital ratio =
RWAs
Eligible Total Capital
Total Capital (CRAR) =
RWAs

In case a bank is set up under a Non-Operative Financial Holding Company (NOFHC),
the NOFHC shall maintain capital adequacy and other requirements on a consolidated
basis as stipulated in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to compute its capital ratios in the following manner: cet1 capital common equity tier 1 (cet1) capital ratio = total risk weighted assets (rwas) eligibl

controls.control_objective: To ensure consist of the sum of the following categories: (1) tier 1 capital (going-concern capital)
controls.control_description:
Total regulatory capital shall consist of the sum of the following categories:
(1) Tier 1 Capital (going-concern capital)
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to consist of the sum of the following categories: (1) tier 1 capital (going-concern capital)

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain a minimum total capital (mtc) of 15 per cent of the risk weighted assets (rwas) on an ongoing basis, i
controls.control_description:
The limits and minimum capital requirements are as under:
(1) A bank shall maintain a minimum total capital (MTC) of 15 per cent of the risk
weighted assets (RWAs) on an ongoing basis, i.e., capital to risk-weighted assets
ratio (CRAR) shall be at least 15 per cent on an ongoing basis.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain a minimum total capital (mtc) of 15 per cent of the risk weighted assets (rwas) on an ongoing basis, i

controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be further divided into different components as described under following paragraphs

controls.control_description: (2) CET1 capital shall be at least 6 per cent of the RWAs on an ongoing basis;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be at least 6 per cent of the rwas on an ongoing basis

controls.control_objective: To ensure be at least 7
controls.control_description: (3) Tier 1 capital shall be at least 7.5 per cent of the RWAs on an ongoing basis.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be at least 7

controls.control_objective: To ensure be 1
controls.control_description:
Thus, within the minimum Tier 1 capital, the maximum AT1 capital that can be
admitted shall be 1.5 per cent of the RWAs;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be 1

controls.control_objective: To ensure be at least 15 per cent of the rwas on an ongoing basis, the maximum tier 2 capital that can be admitted shall be 7
controls.control_description:
(4) As total capital (Tier 1 capital + Tier 2 capital) shall be at least 15 per cent of the
RWAs on an ongoing basis, the maximum Tier 2 capital that can be admitted
shall be 7.5 per cent of the RWAs within the minimum CRAR of 15 per cent.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be at least 15 per cent of the rwas on an ongoing basis, the maximum tier 2 capital that can be admitted shall be 7

controls.control_objective: To ensure be limited to a maximum of 100 per cent of total tier 1 capital
controls.control_description:
Further, Tier 2 capital shall be limited to a maximum of 100 per cent of total Tier
1 capital.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be limited to a maximum of 100 per cent of total tier 1 capital

(1) AFS - Reserve shall be as per the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial

A bank shall not grant

controls.control_objective: To ensure qualify for inclusion within tier 2 capital

unidentified losses, which are freely available to meet losses which
subsequently materialise, shall qualify for inclusion within Tier 2
capital.

controls.control_objective: To ensure qualify for inclusion in tier 2 capital
controls.control_description:
Accordingly, general provisions on standard assets, floating
provisions, incremental provisions in respect of unhedged foreign
currency exposures, provisions held for country exposures, excess
provisions which arise on account of sale of NPAs and 'countercyclical
provisioning buffer' shall qualify for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.

by the bank for this purpose) and fully paid-in;

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used
controls.control_description:
Where '+' or '-' notation is attached to the rating, the corresponding main rating
category risk weight shall be used.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used

controls.control_objective: To ensure be considered to be in the a rating category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight
controls.control_description:
For example, A+ or A- shall be considered to
be in the A rating category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be considered to be in the a rating category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight

controls.control_objective: To ensure also receive a 150 per cent risk weight
controls.control_description:
If an issuer has a long-term exposure with an external long-term rating that
warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent, all unrated claims on the same counter-
party, whether short-term or long-term, shall also receive a 150 per cent risk
weight, unless the bank uses recognised credit risk mitigation techniques for
such claims.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also receive a 150 per cent risk weight

controls.control_objective: To ensure be deemed to be issue- specific
controls.control_description:
For risk-weighting purposes, short-term ratings shall be deemed to be issue-
specific.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be deemed to be issue- specific

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used to derive risk weights for claims arising from the rated facility
controls.control_description:
They shall be used to derive risk weights for claims arising from the
rated facility.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used to derive risk weights for claims arising from the rated facility

controls.control_objective: To ensure be generalised to other short-term claims
controls.control_description: They shall not be generalised to other short-term claims.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be generalised to other short-term claims

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used to support a risk weight for an unrated long-term claim
controls.control_description:
In no event
a short-term rating shall be used to support a risk weight for an unrated long-term
claim.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used to support a risk weight for an unrated long-term claim

controls.control_objective: To ensure only be used for short-term claims against banks and corporates
controls.control_description:
Short-term assessments may only be used for short-term claims against
banks and corporates.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to only be used for short-term claims against banks and corporates

controls.control_objective: To ensure apply
controls.control_description:
Notwithstanding the above restriction on using an issue specific short-term rating
for other short-term exposures, the following broad principles shall apply.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to apply

controls.control_objective: To ensure attract a risk weight of at least one level higher than the risk weight applicable to the rated short-term claim on that counterparty
controls.control_description:
The
unrated short-term claim on counterparty shall attract a risk weight of at least one
level higher than the risk weight applicable to the rated short-term claim on that
counterparty.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to attract a risk weight of at least one level higher than the risk weight applicable to the rated short-term claim on that counterparty

controls.control_objective: To ensure attract a risk weight lower than 30 per cent or 100 per cent respectively
controls.control_description:
If a short-term rated facility to counterparty attracts a 20 per cent
or a 50 per cent risk-weight, unrated short-term claims to the same counterparty
shall not attract a risk weight lower than 30 per cent or 100 per cent respectively.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to attract a risk weight lower than 30 per cent or 100 per cent respectively

controls.control_objective: To ensure also receive a 150 per cent risk weight
controls.control_description:
Similarly, if an issuer has a short-term exposure with an external short-term rating
that warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent, all unrated claims on the same
counter-party, whether long-term or short-term, shall also receive a 150 per cent
risk weight, unless the bank uses recognised credit risk mitigation techniques for
such claims.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also receive a 150 per cent risk weight

category risk weight should be used for A2 and below, unless specified

chosen domestic rating agencies shall be reviewed annually by the Reserve
Bank.

assigned by the agency.

Standardised Approach.

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used to determine the risk weight of the claim
controls.control_description:
(i) If there is only one rating by a chosen credit rating agency for a particular
claim, that rating shall be used to determine the risk weight of the claim.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used to determine the risk weight of the claim

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applied
controls.control_description:
(ii) If there are two ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies that map
into different risk weights, the higher risk weight shall be applied.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applied

controls.control_objective: To ensure be referred to and the higher of those two risk weights shall be applied
controls.control_description:
(iii) If there are three or more ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies
with different risk weights, the ratings corresponding to the two lowest risk
weights shall be referred to and the higher of those two risk weights shall
be applied.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be referred to and the higher of those two risk weights shall be applied

controls.control_objective: To ensure be based on this assessment
controls.control_description:
Where a bank invests in a particular issue that has an issue specific rating by a
chosen credit rating agency the risk weight of the claim shall be based on this
assessment.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be based on this assessment

controls.control_objective: To ensure apply:
controls.control_description:
Where the bank's claim is not an investment in a specific assessed
issue, the following general principles shall apply:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to apply:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applied to the bank's unassessed claim only if this claim ranks pari passu or senior to the specific rated debt in all respects and the maturity of the unassessed claim is not later than the maturity of the rated claim
controls.control_description:
(i) In circumstances where the borrower has a specific assessment for an
issued debt - but the bank's claim is not an investment in this particular debt
- the rating applicable to the specific debt (where the rating maps into a risk
weight lower than that which applies to an unrated claim) may be applied
to the bank's unassessed claim only if this claim ranks pari passu or senior
to the specific rated debt in all respects and the maturity of the unassessed
claim is not later than the maturity of the rated claim, except where the rated
claim is a short term obligation as specified in paragraph 131.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applied to the bank's unassessed claim only if this claim ranks pari passu or senior to the specific rated debt in all respects and the maturity of the unassessed claim is not later than the maturity of the rated claim

controls.control_description:
If not, the
rating applicable to the specific debt cannot be used and the unassessed
claim shall receive the risk weight for unrated claims.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to receive the risk weight for unrated claims

as A1+ and a long-term claim on the same counterparty is rated as AAA,
<image>
then a bank shall assign a 30 per cent risk weight to an unrated short-term
claim and 20 per cent risk weight to an unrated long-term claim on that
counterparty where the seniority of the claim ranks pari-passu with the rated
claims and the maturity of the unrated claim is not later than the rated claim.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to assign a 30 per cent risk weight to an unrated short-term claim and 20 per cent risk weight to an unrated long-term claim on that counterparty where t

controls.control_description:
In a similar case where a short-term claim is rated A1+ and a long-term
claim is rated A, the bank shall assign 50 per cent risk weight to an unrated
short term or long-term claim.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to assign 50 per cent risk weight to an unrated short term or long-term claim

A loan rating
without the above disclosure by the ECAI shall not be eligible for being
reckoned for capital computation by a bank.

applicable in terms of extant instructions.

controls.control_description:

controls.control_description:
If either the issuer or a single issue has

that which applies to unrated claims), an unassessed claim on the same
<image>
counterparty that ranks pari-passu or is subordinated to either the senior
unsecured issuer assessment or the exposure assessment shall be
assigned the same risk weight as is applicable to the low-quality
assessment.

shall be extended to the entire amount of credit risk exposure the bank has

no recognition of credit risk mitigation techniques shall be taken into
account if the credit enhancement is already reflected in the issue specific
rating accorded by a chosen credit rating agency relied upon by the bank.

on account of deposits placed in lieu of shortfall in

(iv) While the use of CRM techniques reduces or transfers credit risk, it

controls.control_description:

controls.control_description:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be calculated as follows: e* = max {0, [e x (1 + he) - c x (1 - hc - hfx)]} where: e* = the exposure value after risk mitigation e = current value of
controls.control_description:
Calculation of capital requirement
(1) For a collateralised transaction, the exposure amount after risk mitigation shall
be calculated as follows:
E* = max {0, [E x (1 + He) - C x (1 - Hc - Hfx)]}
where:
E* = the exposure value after risk mitigation
E = current value of the exposure for which the collateral qualifies as a risk
mitigant
He = haircut appropriate to the exposure
C = the current value of the collateral received
Hc = haircut appropriate to the collateral
Hfx = haircut appropriate for currency mismatch between the collateral and
exposure
<image>
(2) The exposure amount after risk mitigation (i.e., E*) shall be multiplied by the risk
weight of the counterparty to obtain the RWA amount for the collateralised
transaction.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be calculated as follows: e* = max {0, [e x (1 + he) - c x (1 - hc - hfx)]} where: e* = the exposure value after risk mitigation e = current value of

controls.control_description:

The calculation of the counterparty credit risk charge for an individual contract
shall be as follows:
counterparty charge = [(RC + add-on) - CA] x r x 15%
where:
RC = the replacement cost,
add-on = the amount for potential future exposure calculated according to
paragraph 75(2),
CA = the volatility adjusted collateral amount under the comprehensive
approach prescribed in paragraphs 148 and 149 or zero if no eligible collateral
is applied to the transaction, and
r = the risk weight of the counterparty.
